ai_research.headline: OpenAI ships GPT-5.5 (agentic, 2x price) — ICLR 2026 kicks off in Rio with agentic AI now #1 research topic

items.title: OpenAI GPT-5.5 — agentic, same latency, double the price
items.body: GPT-5.5 released as an agentic model that switches between tools autonomously on long-horizon tasks. Per-token latency matches GPT-5.4 but intelligence is a tier higher. Pricing is $5/1M input and $30/1M output — 2x GPT-5.4. If you're running agent loops at any scale, this changes the cost math: test 5.5 on hard tasks only, keep 5.4 for high-volume flows.

items.title: GPT-5.4 Thinking hits 75% on OSWorld-Verified — true OS-level computer use
items.body: GPT-5.4's Thinking variant surpassed human-level on OSWorld-Verified (75.0%) and unlocks native computer use at the OS level. This isn't a screenshot-and-click harness — it's the model operating the machine directly. The 'agent as remote coworker' pattern just got real for production.

items.title: ICLR 2026 opens today in Rio — agentic AI overtakes LLMs as top category
items.body: The Fourteenth ICLR runs Apr 24–28 at Riocentro. 19,797 valid submissions — a 70% jump YoY, now the largest ML venue on the planet. The big shift: agentic AI has officially overtaken passive LLM representations as the dominant research stream. 'Passive Representations' → 'Active Trajectories.' Papers to watch: Google's TurboQuant (vector quant memory fix) and an agent arena with 103K+ battles across 31 scenarios.

items.title: OpenAI open-weights a local PII redaction model
items.body: OpenAI released an open-weight model for detecting and redacting personally identifiable information in text, designed to run locally so PII never leaves the user's machine. Interesting move: first real open-weights ship from OpenAI in a while, and it's a defensive/compliance play, not a flagship. Relevant if QuizzyDots ever ingests user-generated text.

items.title: Anthropic turbulence — Mythos still unreleased, IPO shadow at ~$800B
items.body: Axios reports Anthropic is hitting turbulence ahead of a potential IPO that could value it near $800B. Revenue tripled to $30B this year on coding-tool demand, but unreleased 'Mythos' (ASL-4) still leads their internal leaderboard while Claude Opus 4.7 trails it. OpenAI is actively courting frustrated Anthropic devs ahead of dueling IPOs.

items.title: Sony AI's Ace robot defeats elite human table tennis players — published in Nature
items.body: Sony AI published in Nature (Apr 22) that Ace is the first robot to beat expert human players in a live competitive physical sport. Perceives, decides, and acts faster than human reaction time. Extends their Gran Turismo Sophy arc (virtual → physical). The physical AI frontier is accelerating faster than most people track: this isn't a factory arm or warehouse bot, it's real-time open-world sport.

items.title: ICLR paper watch: LLM-agent token waste & unstable execution
items.body: Multiple accepted arXiv papers this week tackle the same pain points: high reasoning overhead, excessive token consumption, unstable execution, and inability to reuse components across agent runs. Relevant for OpenClaw/MCP — the field is converging on reusable agent components as the bottleneck, not model IQ.

ai_research.learn_today: Stress-test GPT-5.5 on one of your hardest agent loops — then decide whether the 2x token cost is worth the intelligence bump, or whether 5.4 at half price still wins.

openclaw_agents.headline: OS-level computer use goes mainstream; TikTok growth moves from generative → agentic

items.title: Native OS computer use is the new baseline
items.body: With GPT-5.4 Thinking at 75% OSWorld and 5.5 shipping agentic by default, screenshot-based harnesses are about to feel dated. OpenClaw-style agents that talk to OS primitives directly (file system, window manager, processes) have a structural advantage — less brittle than vision-click loops.
items.try_this: Pick one OpenClaw flow that still uses vision-click and port it to direct OS calls. Measure latency and failure rate before/after.

items.title: Agentic TikTok tools > generative TikTok tools
items.body: Notable shift in the TikTok tool market: 'generative' tools (make me a caption) are losing share to 'agentic' tools that operate on goals ('grow my account 10% this month') and chain actions. NoimosAI + AI Toker are the names to know — AI Toker scans 10K+ viral videos daily for hook patterns. Directly applicable to QuizzyDots TikTok strategy.
items.try_this: Spend 30 min reviewing what AI Toker surfaces for 'quiz / trivia' hooks this week. Steal the top 2 hook patterns for the next batch of slideshows.

items.title: Revealbot for cross-platform ad automation
items.body: Revealbot runs rule-based automation across Meta, Google, TikTok, and Snapchat — 'if CPA > X for 2 days, pause; if ROAS > Y, scale 20%.' Saves hours of manual campaign babysitting. Not sexy, but the kind of infrastructure that compounds for anyone running paid on QuizzyDots or InstaClaw.
items.try_this: Draft 3 simple rules you'd want auto-enforced on your current paid campaigns. Even if you don't buy the tool, the rule-set is useful.

items.title: TikTok Smart+ App Campaigns update
items.body: TikTok Ads Manager added another pass of Smart+ improvements: AI optimizes bids, creatives, and targeting simultaneously for app campaigns. Lower skill floor to get decent performance — but also means the creative itself becomes the only real lever you control.
items.try_this: If you're running Smart+ on QuizzyDots, strip it to 3 creative variants and let the AI do its thing for 7 days. Stop A/B testing inside the same campaign.

openclaw_agents.try_today: Port one OpenClaw vision-click flow to direct OS calls and measure the latency delta — the OSWorld result means this is about to be table stakes.

tesla.note: Cybercab production officially started — Musk posted on X this morning. TSLA barely moving pre-market despite the milestone; market wants revenue, not tweets. Dallas/Houston unsupervised robotaxi (launched Apr 18) still the real catalyst — $3.25 base + $1/mi. The other 5 announced cities (Phoenix, Miami, Orlando, Tampa, Vegas) quietly slipped from '1H 2026' to 'preparations underway.'

tesla_spacex_xai.spacex: Starship Flight 12 (V3 debut) slipped from late-April to NET early-to-mid May. Ship 39 + Booster 19 both done with full static fires and wet dress rehearsals; Pad 2 upgrades near complete. V3 targets 100+ tons to LEO, ~3x V2. Third slip this year, so don't bet on exact dates — but hardware is genuinely ready.
tesla_spacex_xai.xai: Quiet week for xAI on public news — Grok-3 preview still dominating dev chatter from earlier this month. No fresh drops from @xai or @elonmusk in the last 72h that moved the conversation.

items.title: Michael
items.note: Antoine Fuqua's MJ biopic opens today. Jaafar Jackson (MJ's nephew) leads, Colman Domingo co-stars. Fuqua usually delivers on tone — worth the theater trip.

items.title: Marty Supreme
items.note: Timothée Chalamet as a ping-pong prodigy. A24's highest-grossing film ever ($178M worldwide), 9 Oscar nominations. Now on HBO Max — the obvious weekend watch.

items.title: Fuze
items.note: WWII unexploded bomb unearthed in modern London triggers citywide evacuation. Aaron Taylor-Johnson, Theo James, Sam Worthington. Cool premise, flat execution per early reviews.

items.title: Apex
items.note: Charlize Theron vs. Taron Egerton in a wilderness survival thriller on Netflix. Wait for RT — too many generic action-thriller titles this year to gamble a Friday night on.

items.title: Half Man
items.note: Richard Gadd's follow-up to Baby Reindeer dropped yesterday (Apr 23). If Baby Reindeer wrecked you, this is non-negotiable.

items.title: NoimosAI — goal-based TikTok growth agent
items.body: Instead of 'write me a caption,' you give it 'grow followers 10% this month' and it executes the steps — posting cadence, hook testing, hashtag rotation. Modular with a dedicated Growth Metrics Agent. Exactly the kind of tool to point at QuizzyDots and let run.

items.title: AI Toker — daily viral hook scanner
items.body: Scans 10,000+ viral videos per day and surfaces the hooks and sounds currently gaining traction. Not a creation tool — a research tool. 15 min/day with this beats guessing at hooks from scratch.

items.title: Revealbot — cross-platform ad automation
items.body: Rule-based automation across Meta, Google, TikTok, Snapchat. 'If CPA > X for 2 days, pause.' 'If ROAS > Y, scale 20%.' Unglamorous infrastructure but the kind of leverage that compounds over months.

items.title: TikTok Smart+ App Campaigns
items.body: TikTok's native AI campaign type for apps — optimizes bids, creatives, and targeting in one loop. Low skill floor, which means creative quality is now the only real differentiator inside Smart+.

macro.mood: Muted Friday tape. TSLA flat despite the Cybercab production start — market shrugging at Musk posts. BTC nudging green (+1%) ahead of your weekly $10 DCA. LMND quietly leading the pack (+1%). Nothing exciting, which is fine — boring Fridays are usually followed by signal-rich weekends.
